Research Toolkit
CDSS License Lookup

Riverside County Procurement

This Week's Content Ideas
Reads what's actually in your pipeline right now -- org types, cities, common objections, pain points from logged calls -- and writes personalized content for your LinkedIn, Instagram, and blog. Not generic advice. Built from your own leads.
Quick Log Formatter
Paste voice transcription, typed notes, or a messy call summary. Formats it into clean CRM-ready notes with a copy button for each field.
Paste your call notes or voice transcription below.
Ready to paste into CRM
Cowork Bot Instructions
Copy the full instruction set into Cowork. Task A runs weekly Monday morning. Task B runs when you have DM conversations that did not convert. Task C runs quarterly. Task D runs monthly.
COUSINS STYLING STUDIO -- COWORK STANDING TASKS ================================================= TASK A: WEEKLY OUTREACH EXECUTION Run every Monday morning before calls start. Context: Cousins Styling Studio is an institutional mobile hair care and professional appearance programming company in Moreno Valley, CA. The goal is $2,500/month in recurring contracts. Current revenue is $500-$1,500/month. The bottleneck is converting reached decision makers into booked discovery calls. There are two programs: the Recurring Experience Program (senior living, adult residential, adult day care) and the Confidence and Independence Program (FFAs, adult day programs, workforce clients). Step 1 -- Pull the priority contact list. From the Prime Targets tab in the CRM, filter to Tier: Now, Stage: Not Contacted or Contacted. List every organization where no email has been sent yet. These are the week's primary targets. Step 2 -- Build personalized emails for each Now-tier target. For each organization, research the following before writing: a. Go to their website and identify what population they serve. b. Find the program director, activities director, or executive director name if not already in the CRM. c. Find their email if not already in the CRM. d. Note any recent news, funding announcements, or program language that you can reference. Then write a personalized email using this structure: - Subject: use something specific to their org, NOT a generic subject like "Hair Care Services" - Opening line: reference something specific about their program or population. Not "I hope this finds you well." - One sentence on what Cousins does and who it serves. - One sentence on which specific program applies to their organization and why. - One sentence on what the next step is (Discovery Call, no commitment). - Signature: Jordyn Brown, Cousins Styling Studio, (951) 653-4477, info@bookcousins.com Step 3 -- Build the follow-up sequence for previously contacted organizations. From the CRM Contacted tab, identify every record where: - Stage is Decision Maker Contacted or Decision Maker Identified - Last call log entry is more than 5 business days ago - No Program Overview has been sent yet These organizations need a follow-up email this week. Use the standard Program Overview email but personalize the opening line based on the call log note. Step 4 -- Identify the 10 calls to make today. Priority order: 1. Any record with a scheduled follow-up date of today or earlier. 2. Now-tier Prime Targets with no call log at all. 3. Priority A CRM records in stage Decision Maker Identified with no next action date set. 4. Any record where the last log says "Voicemail Left" and it has been 3+ business days. List the 10 in order and include the phone number and who to ask for. Step 5 -- Deliver a daily briefing. Format: CALLS TO MAKE TODAY (10): 1. [Org name] -- [phone] -- ask for [DM name] -- context: [one line from notes] ... EMAILS TO SEND TODAY: [Org] -- [email] -- [one line on what to personalize] ... FOLLOW-UPS DUE: [Org] -- [what was promised] -- [due date] --- TASK B: DM CONVERSION -- TURNING "I'LL PASS IT ALONG" INTO A MEETING Run when Jordyn reports she reached a decision maker but did not get a meeting booked. Context: The current bottleneck is converting DM conversations into booked discovery calls. This happens because the DM hears the pitch but does not feel enough urgency or specificity to pull out their calendar on the spot. The fix is not more volume -- it is a better follow-up sequence that creates a specific reason to meet. Step 1 -- Identify who was reached but did not book. Look at the CRM for records where: - Stage is Decision Maker Contacted - There is no meeting scheduled - Last log entry is within the past 7 days List them. Step 2 -- For each one, write a same-day follow-up email. This email goes out within 2 hours of the call. Structure: - Subject: "Following up on our conversation -- [Org Name]" - Line 1: Thank them for the specific time they gave you. Reference something they said. If they said they serve adults with developmental disabilities, say that. - Line 2: One sentence on what you are sending them and why it is relevant to their specific population. - Line 3: The Program Overview link: https://cousinsstylingstudio.github.io/scale-proposal/ - Line 4: A specific ask. Not "let me know if you have questions." Instead: "I have time on [Tuesday] or [Thursday] for a 20-minute Discovery Call. Would either work for you?" Give two specific options. - Line 5: One sentence on what the Discovery Call covers (no commitment, just a conversation to see if there is a fit). Step 3 -- Schedule the follow-up if they do not respond. If no response in 5 business days, prepare a second follow-up that: - References the original email with a one-line summary. - Adds one new piece of value -- a peer example, a specific stat, or a relevant program outcome. - Repeats the specific two-time-slot ask. Step 4 -- Flag any DM who has been in Decision Maker Contacted for more than 14 days. These are stalling. Write a "break-up" email for each one. The break-up email says you are going to stop reaching out unless they would like to reconnect, and gives them one clear CTA. Stalled leads take up mental bandwidth that should go to new targets. --- TASK C: QUARTERLY PRIME TARGET REFRESH Run once per quarter (every 90 days from the last refresh date). Step 1 -- Check procurement sources for anything new. - Riverside County Statement of Proceedings: https://rivcocob.org/2024-agendas-and-proceedings (navigate to the current year page) - San Bernardino County Board Actions: search sbcounty.gov Board Actions for the latest entries - Riverside County open bids: https://purchasing.co.riverside.ca.us/open-bids Filter for DPSS, Behavioral Health, and Workforce Development only. Skip IT, fleet, construction trades. Step 2 -- Check on Later-tier facilities. - RUHS Wellness Village, Mead Valley: search for opening date or announcement - Harmony Haven Children and Youth Wellness Center: search for construction progress or opening timeline - San Bernardino Behavioral Health Victorville Campus: search for construction progress - County Foster Youth Campus (SB County): check whether this has moved from proposed to funded If any Later-tier facility shows signs of opening within the next two quarters, flag it for promotion to Now tier. Step 3 -- Identify new award winners. Look for new multi-year professional services contract ratifications in foster care, residential care, or behavioral health. Distinguish live contracts from one-time ARPA or relief funding. One-time grants belong in Later tier only if they are recent. Skip anything older than two years. Step 4 -- Deliver a refresh summary. Answer three questions: 1. What moved from Later to Now this quarter and why. 2. What new targets were found and what is the evidence for each. 3. What is unchanged and does not need attention until next quarter. --- TASK D: BUILD-TO-SELL DOCUMENTATION Run once per month. This task generates the documentation an acquirer or investor would want to see. Context: Cousins is building toward a sellable operation. A founder-dependent service business sells for 1-2x revenue. A documented, systemized operation with recurring contracts, a trained team, and a replicable sales process sells for 3-5x or more. Every month of clean documentation increases the exit value. Step 1 -- Pull the month's outreach activity from the CRM. Report: - Total organizations contacted this month - Total calls made (count of call log entries) - Total emails sent - Conversion rate from first contact to DM reached - Conversion rate from DM reached to meeting booked - Conversion rate from meeting to proposal - Conversion rate from proposal to contract Step 2 -- Summarize the pipeline. - Total organizations in active pipeline (not Kill Zone, not Not Contacted) - Pipeline value: sum of ACV for all Contract Active records times 12 - Estimated pipeline value: sum of ACV estimates for Verbal Yes and Proposal Sent records - Number of organizations in each stage Step 3 -- Produce a one-page monthly operations report. Format suitable for sharing with an advisor, investor, or potential acquirer. Include: MRR, pipeline value, outreach volume, conversion rates, notable wins, and one risk or challenge. Step 4 -- Flag anything that breaks the repeatable system. If Jordyn is the only person who can execute a step, flag it. If a process relies on her memory rather than the CRM, flag it. The goal is to identify and eliminate founder-dependency one piece at a time.

📧 Email Directory
How To Use

CRM Tab

Search, filter, and work individual facility records. Every org shows a colored left border and stage badge by pipeline heat. Green = active contract. Violet = verbal yes. Gold/amber = meeting or proposal. Blue = DM reached. Log every call. Save notes. Mark stage after each interaction.

🔥 Pipeline Tab

All contacted orgs ranked highest to lowest by pipeline heat. Filter by Hot (meeting+), DM Reached, VM/No Answer, Overdue (7+ days untouched), or All. Click any card to open the full record. Use this view to run your daily call session.

✓ Contacted Tab

Full list of every org reached, with stage chips you can click to filter. Shows DM info, last contact, follow-up dates, and notes preview. Click any name to open the record.

Prime Targets Tab

Organizations holding live county contracts. Now tier = call this week. Later tier = check quarterly. Stale warning fires after 100 days. Click any row to add contact info and notes.

Emails Tab

Every email found across all records. Filter to Not Sent Yet to see who to email next. DM Field emails are editable inline. Non-DM emails have a Save to DM button. Mark Sent after each goes out. Red X moves the org to Kill Zone without leaving the tab.

⚡ Outreach Engine Tab

Daily tracker, priority queue, follow-up alerts, research toolkit, and the full Cowork instructions. Copy the instructions into Cowork. Task A runs Monday morning. Task B runs after DM conversations that did not convert. Task C runs quarterly. Task D runs monthly for build-to-sell documentation.

Kill Zone

Red Kill Zone button on any record removes it from active counts without deleting data. Use for wrong numbers, closed facilities, and duplicates. Restore any time from the Kill Zone tab.

💾 Backup and Restore

After every session: click the green Save Backup JSON button in the top bar. This downloads a .json file with all your CRM data, follow-ups, and Kill Zone records. Name it with the date. Store it somewhere safe.

To restore after a browser clear: upload the .json file using the Import feature (coming) or share it with the coder to re-inject as the new preload. The CRM shows the last backup date in the top bar so you always know how far back your data goes.

After importing a JOS export file: check the top bar for the last backup date. Enter any calls logged after that date manually. JOS exports are always in the exported Session Notes format.

📞 Phone Call Recording (Roadmap)

Recording integration is planned. The approach: record calls via a third-party dialer (Aircall, JustCall, or Google Voice with auto-record), save recordings to a shared folder, and use Cowork or a transcription service to pull objections, DM names, and follow-up dates from the transcript directly into CRM records. This removes the manual logging step entirely.

🤖 Cowork Automation (Roadmap)

Current Cowork tasks are copy-paste. The next phase connects Cowork directly to the CRM via a shared JSON file or API, allowing Cowork to: pull the priority queue automatically each Monday, generate personalized emails for each org from their notes and call history, update stages after confirmed contact, and scrape procurement sources on a quarterly schedule without manual prompting.

...
🔥 Pipeline
Every contacted org ranked by pipeline heat. Click any name to open the full record.
🔍 Scrape Results
Every research batch organized by date and source. Contact info, call angles, next steps, and one-click add to Prime Targets or CRM.
